config.purpose: Re-running Table 3 (cross-baseline native-format decode latency) cells that were `--` or `†` in the paper draft.

results.note: v1 data showed B=32 always crashed; current run succeeds — kernel must have been fixed since v1 measurement, or v1 had memory pressure.

results.error: CUDA error: an illegal memory access was encountered (kernel limitation at N>=32K, not wall-time)

CUDA error: an illegal memory access was encountered (qjl_quant_kernel.cu:79 int-indexing: bhn*group*emb = 4194304*4*128 = 2^31 overflows int)
results.theoretical_method: 32 x B=1 N=16K single-call latency; in-process sequential batching also fails (second call CUDA illegal mem at N=16K)

summary.vs_fastest_non_cda_unchanged: Headline ratios are unchanged (FA2 remains fastest non-CDA in every Table 3 row).
